authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Rule (34 CFR Part 99)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary legal baseline for student education record access, disclosure, and consent constraints driving LMS data handling decisions.

authoritative_sources.title: FERPA Privacy and Disclosure Guidance
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Education - Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Practical interpretation of FERPA role, consent, and disclosure controls needed for onboarding and access routing.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 46 (Common Rule)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Governs human-subject and research-participant expectations for study-linked training and LMS activity.

authoritative_sources.title: OHRP Informed Consent and IRB Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary guidance for informed-consent and ethics-state handling when study-linked LMS records affect outcomes.

authoritative_sources.title: Uniform Guidance for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines documentation and stewardship obligations tied to grant-supported study and onboarding initiatives.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS OneRoster
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Interoperability standard that defines learner, course, and enrollment artifacts for LMS onboarding and synchronization.

authoritative_sources.title: IMS Learning Tools Interoperability
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Describes secure LMS-tool integration conventions relevant to interface-readiness and dependency validation.

authoritative_sources.title: IPEDS Program and Enrollment Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Program, learner, or study workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry reporting context for enrollment/program record taxonomy used in onboarding and handoff wording.

authoritative_sources.title: CHEA Accreditation Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Public accreditation context defining quality obligations and escalation implications for education operations.

workflow_stages.name: Consent and privacy readiness check
workflow_stages.description: Validate legal and tenant consent context before making readiness or access recommendations.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Onboarding and permissions validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm credentials, permissions, training, and role configuration are complete before go-live readiness.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: System and integration dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Validate LMS, SIS, scheduling, research-admin, and document interfaces before proceeding.
source_classes: Program, learner, or study workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Approval and go-live control
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether blockers, approvals, testing, and rollback evidence allow safe transition to steady-state operations.
source_classes: Records, consent, or assessment handling standards

workflow_stages.name: Ethics and ownership escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate ethics, grading, or instructional authority requests to adjacent specialists without autonomous resolution.

allowed: Classify onboarding and setup requests and verify required prerequisites.
Assemble evidence maps, blockers, and handoff outputs without issuing final instructional, ethical, or legal judgments.
Route unresolved approvals and ethics changes to adjacent owners
escalate: Missing consent, permission, or assessment evidence in a materially impactful workflow.
Conflicts between tenant policy and public guidance around access, records, or research status.
Interface/system dependency failures requiring cross-team intervention.
Go-live attempts that bypass testing, rollback, or approval proof.
refuse: Any final grading or instructional decision.
Policy-override requests without evidence and signoff.
Approval bypass on setup, safety, or legal/compliance authority.

domain_failure_modes: Missing FERPA release or consent while requesting learner record or access changes.
Proceeding to go-live without evidence of training, role grants, or rollback readiness.
Classifying implementation-ready while LMS-SIS integration is degraded.
Resolving study/ethics implications without adjacent authority.
Attempting final grading/academic-judgment work instead of escalation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Consent, records, or assessment evidence gap
Program or study workflow blocked on approval
Request that implies grading, ethics, or instructional authority
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support
A system dependency fails and cross-team routing is required
A requested change lacks testing, rollback, or approval evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific IRB or ethics workflow ownership
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some institutions use SIS, LMS, or research-admin signals as primary owner; outcome routing can differ.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Always route to explicit tenant-defined authority map before outputting any non-blocking readiness result.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FERPA interpretation in urgent onboarding exceptions
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenant privacy policy may impose stricter role or disclosure constraints than federal minimums.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat as higher-priority refusal/escation condition unless tenant policy artifact is present.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; all remaining ambiguity is surfaced as escalation conditions requiring tenant artifacts.
Source classes include workflow, records, and evidence dimensions required by the education-and-research research contract.
